heading: Supporting the identification and recovery of abducted Ukrainian children
label: 8926a Supporting the identification and recovery of abducted Ukrainian children
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2026, and not as part of the Ukraine Freedom Support Act of 2014, which comprises this chapter.

children.heading: Short title
children.label: a Short title
children.text: This section may be cited as the “Abducted Ukrainian Children Recovery and Accountability Act”.

According to a White House press release, dated , “The United States and Ukraine agreed that the United States remains committed to helping achieve the exchange of prisoners of war, the release of civilian detainees, and the return of forcibly transferred Ukrainian children.”.March 25, 2025

children.label: A
children.text: investigations of Russia’s abduction of Ukrainian children;

children.label: B
children.text: the rehabilitation and reintegration of children returned to Ukraine; and

children.label: C
children.text: justice and accountability for perpetrators of the abductions.

children.heading: Authorization of technical assistance and advisory support
children.label: c Authorization of technical assistance and advisory support
A
children.text: to provide law enforcement and intelligence technical assistance, training, capacity building, and advisory support to the Government of Ukraine in support of the commitment described in subsection (b)(1); and

children.label: B
children.text: to advance the objectives described in subsection (b)(2).

children.label: A
children.text: training regarding the utilization of biometric identification technologies in abduction and trafficking in persons investigations;

children.label: B
children.text: assistance with respect to collecting and analyzing open source intelligence information;

children.label: C
children.text: assistance in the development and use of secure communications technologies; and

children.label: D
children.text: assistance with respect to managing and securing relevant databases.

children.label: A
children.text: the amount of assistance determined to be obligated;

children.label: B
children.text: the type of assistance to be utilized; and

children.label: C
children.text: any information on the technology operationalized to support the means identified in this subsection.

children.heading: Nongovernmental organizations
children.label: 1 Nongovernmental organizations
children.text: The Department of Justice and the Department of State may coordinate with nongovernmental organizations to carry out the assistance authorized under subsection (c).

children.heading: Federal agencies
children.label: 2 Federal agencies
children.text: The National Security Council may coordinate with appropriate representatives from the Department of Justice, the Department of State, the intelligence community (as defined in ), and other Federal agencies, as needed, to carry out the assistance authorized under subsection (c).section 3003 of title 50

children.label: A
children.text: medical and psychological rehabilitation services;

children.label: B
children.text: family reunification and support services; and

children.label: C
children.text: services in support of the reintegration of such children into Ukrainian society, including case management, legal aid, and educational screening and placement.

children.heading: Report
children.label: 2 Report
children.text: Not later than 60 days after , the Secretary of State shall submit a report to the Committee on Foreign Relations of the Senate and the Committee on Foreign Affairs of the House of Representatives that describes all current or planned foreign assistance programs that will provide the assistance authorized under paragraph (1).December 18, 2025

children.heading: Atrocity Crimes Advisory Group for Ukraine
children.label: f Atrocity Crimes Advisory Group for Ukraine
children.text: The Department of State is authorized to support the Atrocity Crimes Advisory Group for Ukraine by providing technical assistance, capacity building, and advisory support to the Government of Ukraine’s Office of the Prosecutor General, and other relevant components of the Government of Ukraine, for the purpose of investigating and prosecuting cases involving abducted children, and other atrocity crimes.

children.heading: Department of Justice
children.label: g Department of Justice
children.text: The Department of Justice is authorized to provide technical assistance, capacity building, and advisory support to the Government of Ukraine through its Office of Overseas Prosecutorial Development, Assistance, and Training, which shall be coordinated by the Resident Legal Adviser at the United States Embassy in Kyiv, for the purpose of investigating and prosecuting cases involving abducted children, and other atrocity crimes.

the Secretary of State, in coordination with the Attorney General, shall submit a report to the Committee on Foreign Relations of the Senate, the Committee on the Judiciary of the Senate, the Committee on Foreign Affairs of the House of Representatives, and the Committee on the Judiciary of the House of Representatives that describes current and planned United States Government support for the Government of Ukraine’s work to investigate and prosecute atrocity crimes; and

children.label: A
children.text: any discrepancies between the sanctions regimes of the United States, the United Kingdom, and the European Union with respect to those responsible for the abduction of Ukrainian children; and

children.label: B
children.text: efforts made by the United States Government to better align such sanction regimes.
